title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted  where necessary

input_row.title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted  where necessary
input_row.description: Service accounts tokens should not be mounted in pods except where the workload running in the pod explicitly needs to communicate with the API server
input_row.rationale: Mounting service account tokens inside pods can provide an avenue for privilege escalation attacks where an attacker is able to compromise a single pod in the cluster. Avoiding mounting these tokens removes this attack avenue.
input_row.audit: Review pod and service account objects in the cluster and ensure that the option below is set, unless the resource explicitly requires this access. automountServiceAccountToken: false
input_row.remediation: Modify the definition of pods and service accounts which do not need to mount service account tokens to disable it.
input_row.impact: Pods mounted without service account tokens will not be able to communicate with the API server, except where the resource is available to unauthenticated principals.
input_row.default_value: By default, all pods get a service account token mounted in them.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_pod_service_account_token_mount_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands to list all pods and their associated service accounts in the GKE cluster.
- For each pod, specifically check the `automountServiceAccountToken` property in the pod definition, and ensure that it is set to `false` if the pod does not require it.
- Relevant gcloud command to enumerate pods: 
  ```bash
  gcloud container clusters get-credentials [CLUSTER_NAME] --zone [ZONE]
  kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json
  ```
- Validate the configuration properties:
  - For each pod, check if the `automountServiceAccountToken` field is explicitly set to `false`.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The `automountServiceAccountToken` is set to `false` for pods that do not need service account tokens.
  - **Fail:** The `automountServiceAccountToken` is not set to `false` for pods that do not require service account tokens, indicating potential risk of privilege escalation.
- For service accounts, use the following gcloud command:
  ```bash
  kubectl get serviceaccounts --all-namespaces -o json
  ```
- Ensure that service accounts linked to the pods do not have unnecessary permissions related to service account tokens. 

This approach fully leverages GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK commands to automate the audit of the compliance control for service account token management in Google Kubernetes Engine.
